source: Hengaw / HRANA / Iran Health Ministry / Iran International / Al Jazeera / Iran Red Crescent
note: Hengaw 7th report (Mar 25): 6,530 total killed incl. 640 confirmed civilians. HRANA (Day 34): 3,461+ killed incl. 1,551 civilians, 244 children; 8 civilians killed Apr 1 alone (14 injured). Iran International (Apr 1): 4,700+ IRGC/security forces killed. Iran Red Crescent: 24,800+ injured. WHO: 18 hospitals struck; 190+ health/medical centers hit. 3.2M Iranians displaced (UNHCR). Internet blackout 840+ hrs cumulative (~1% connectivity). Former FM Kharazi wounded (wife killed) in Tehran strike Apr 1. 11,000+ targets struck since Feb 28.
Schoolgirls and staff killed in missile strike on primary school. Pentagon preliminary assessment (Mar 12) suggests US at fault; formal investigation opened.

Includes Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei (Feb 28). Ali Larijani (National Security Council Secretary) confirmed killed Mar 17–18 per IRGC. Mojtaba Khamenei status: reportedly wounded, mother/wife/son reportedly killed (unverified). Netanyahu: 'More than half of Iran's most senior leadership' eliminated. Figures are evolving.

Pentagon official: 13 KIA, 300+ wounded. CONTESTED: The Intercept (Apr 1-2) alleges ~750 US troops killed or wounded — claims Hegseth ordered restrictions on casualty reporting. Pentagon denied as 'completely fabricated'; Congressional Democrats requested IG investigation. Official 13 KIA: 6 Army Reserve (Kuwait, Feb 28); Sgt. Pennington (Mar 8); 6 KC-135 crew (Iraq, Mar 13). Mar 28: 15 wounded at Prince Sultan AB. USS Tripoli (3,500 Marines) in theater.

source: IDF Spokesperson / Israeli health authorities / Times of Israel / Al Jazeera
note: 20+ killed in Israel from Iranian/Hezbollah attacks; 6,000+ wounded total. 5 IDF soldiers killed in southern Lebanon fighting in last two days. IDF conducting ground operations in southern Lebanon. Mar 28: Iranian missile struck Eshtaol (Jerusalem area) — interception failure. Mar 27: 1 IDF soldier killed by Lebanese rockets. Mar 24: Iranian missile in Tel Aviv — 3 injured. Mar 18: Elderly couple killed in Ramat Gan. Hezbollah has conducted 1,388 attack waves since March 2.

source: UAE Ministry of Defense / UAE authorities / Reuters
note: Day 32 (Mar 31): Iran struck Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at Port of Dubai — fire and oil spill risk; crew casualties not yet confirmed. Mar 18: UAE Shah Gas Field attacked — operations suspended. Mar 17: 1 Palestinian civilian killed in Abu Dhabi; Dubai airport fuel tank struck. Earlier: 2 armed forces officers killed in helicopter crash (Mar 9). 4 killed and 112+ injured from Iranian strikes to Mar 16. Mar 29: IRGC struck Emirates Global Aluminium — 6 workers wounded.

source: Kuwait Ministry of Health / US State Department
note: Mar 17: Iranian ballistic missile struck US Embassy Kuwait — Rubio ordered closure (no US fatalities from embassy strike). Earlier: 2 Kuwaiti killed from Iranian projectiles.

source: Lebanese Ministry of Health / Lebanese health authorities / Al Jazeera / PBS NewsHour
note: 2,196+ killed incl. 130+ children; 3,315+ injured; 1M+ displaced. Apr 17: 10-day US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah took effect. Displaced families began returning to find severe destruction — Beirut's Haret Hreik hit 62 times over 6 weeks. IDF maintains deployments south of Litani River. Ceasefire expires Apr 22.
Qatar intercepted Iranian missile on Mar 17; blasts heard in Doha. Qatar LNG force majeure risk flagged. Qatar FM warned Gulf states face Iranian retaliation if US ends war unilaterally.

Includes 4 Popular Mobilization Forces fighters killed; 3 injured. Mar 18: Drone attack on US Embassy Baghdad (ongoing). Abu Ali al-Askari (Kata'ib Hezbollah security chief) killed in Mar 14 Baghdad airstrike — announced Mar 17.

Chief Warrant Officer Arnaud Frion, 7th Battalion of Chasseurs Alpins (Varces), killed in Iranian drone strike on joint French-Peshmerga base at Mala Qara, Makhmour district (40km from Erbil), March 13. First French combat fatality.

1 Indian crew member killed aboard MV Safesea Vishnu (Basra, Mar 13); 1 crew killed off Oman coast (Mar 1); 1 other in earlier Hormuz attack. 25+ crew rescued in Basra attacks. 16 ships struck in Gulf total. Hormuz traffic nearly doubled Day 19 as Iran allows more ships.
At least 32 injured (including children) from Iranian strike on Sitra island; Bapco refinery struck causing force majeure; 1 fatality from earlier intercepted missile debris.

source: Saudi Civil Defense Agency / Pentagon / Associated Press
note: Two foreign nationals (Indian and Bangladeshi) killed in earlier Iranian strike on Al-Kharj compound. Mar 28: Iran struck Prince Sultan AB (Al-Kharj) — 15 US service members wounded (5 seriously), refueling aircraft damaged. Mar 29: Saudi forces intercepted 10 Iranian drones. Aramco infrastructure on high alert. Saudi Arabia considering joining coalition strikes (Bloomberg).
Fatality and injuries from projectile hitting a tanker off Muscat coast.
Jordan targeted by 119 Iranian missiles and drones since Feb 28. No fatalities reported.
Protesters stormed US Consulate; security forces opened fire.
